title: Chapter 67 – Fame (2)
content:
Accumulating heterogeneous true qi was, by nature, a dangerous act. They called it cultivation madness.

One could never predict what collision different types of internal energy might cause within the meridians.

Jeong Yeon-shin was different. He could immediately recirculate and alter the nature of any true qi passing through his body.

It was because he had been born with true qi control. This was precisely why he could absorb spirit medicines like replenishment pills and the Great Violet Pill with such ease.

He had already stripped out even the verses of madness. It meant there would be no side effects from using the Demon Roaring Blood Technique.

TL Note- Initiation here is referring to the early mastery of the blood technique.

Surprise spread across the Seventh Apostle's face.

Those who practiced extreme demonic heterodox martial arts could not help but be sensitive to similar techniques.

The characteristics were unmistakable. It meant they could immediately sense a superior art.

Regular members' Blood Summoning Technique was designed to be suppressed by the Apostles' Demon Roaring Blood Technique.

And this was an evil sect group, after all.
Blood Flame Sect members began to bow. The sound of knees striking the ground echoed from every direction.

Desperate cries burst forth everywhere. The atmosphere was solemn.

It was because hearts born of fanaticism infused every shout with raw yearning.

Things had not gone as Gao Zong-xue intended. The platform meant for inciting expectations had become a stage for praise and supplication.

Jeong Yeon-shin stood in silence, the Demon Roaring Blood Technique circulating through him.

The momentum of a superior blood art—calm, with the madness excised.

The sect members widened their eyes as if to engrave even the faintest flutter of the blood-colored robes into memory.

Did he appear noble in their eyes?

In this moment, Jeong Yeon-shin was the Blood Flame Sect's Grand Teacher. From the evil sect's perspective, he appeared entirely so.

He turned sideways without a word. No regular member dared approach.

He ignored Gao Zong-xue's glaring gaze as well, his own mouth sealed shut.

Only the Seventh Apostle followed immediately.

Spirit light—the gleam unique to masters—flickered in her red eye.

Jeong Yeon-shin glanced at her briefly before turning his gaze ahead.

Then he spoke, slowly and deliberately.

He stopped walking abruptly and looked at the Seventh Apostle.

She met his gaze without flinching, her heat-filled outer eye unwavering.
Jeong Yeon-shin said.

Though Jeong Yeon-shin had finished, the Seventh Apostle was practically panting. She appeared deeply excited.

Was she feeling ecstasy? He had merely used the excuse of presenting properly tailored martial arts.

'Indeed. She sees me as prey already caught.'

Words he had assumed would be rejected by a madwoman had worked.

He gained freedom of movement. It seemed he could go anywhere, so long as the Seventh Apostle accompanied him.

Jeong Yeon-shin spoke while moving his feet without hesitation.

'I believe in the main sect, but I need to take measures of my own.'

Realistically, the forces Desolate Sect could mobilize on short notice would not be large.

The murim never lacked for incidents. They said it was rare for even three-tenths of the Divine Sword Squad's seventeen great forces to be gathered at the main family at once.

They were spread across the Central Plains, carrying out missions.

He was inside the Blood Flame Sect. He had to do everything within his power.
He said to the Seventh Apostle.
She nodded and stood still. She was strangely obedient. Her single eye held Jeong Yeon-shin in red.

It was the thirty-six steps leading up to the Sect Leader's hall. Blood Swordmasters who appeared to be on guard offered brief respects.

He simply nodded in acknowledgment.

Jeong Yeon-shin raised his true qi while placing his hand on the railing.

He could not conceal the spreading energy waves. But he had already provided his explanation.

The reason for his capture by the Blood Flame Sect had become justification for moments exactly like this.

The talent for creating martial arts could rationalize any action as inspiration.

'Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm.'

Just as dharma power was about to rise, Jeong Yeon-shin rerouted his energy circulation to the Demon Roaring Blood Technique.

WOONG.

The true qi shifted in a strange way. Deep within the blood technique energy, the qualities of the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm were embedded.

It was power control reaching the realm of divine skill. He took a step forward.

This was the domain of adjusting the intimate structure of true qi with senses honed over a thousand years of lineage.

'Though it does not affect the magnitude of power.'

In short, it was deception. The Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm was wrapped in a shell of blood technique energy.

So that even supreme masters could not fathom its true nature.

He leaned slightly on the railing and raised his hand. Jeong Yeon-shin asked casually:

A Blood Swordmaster standing nearby answered respectfully.

He nodded absently while stroking the railing. Simultaneously, he planted the power of the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm disguised as blood technique true qi.

That was not the end. He drew upon inspiration gained from the Two Flame Blood Spirit Formation that enveloped the Blood Flame Sect headquarters.

It was a formation technique. He locked it so the infused energy would not dissipate.

The power melded in immediately, sinking deep until it no longer emitted even the faintest energy wave.

He said while caressing the smoothly carved wall surface.

He could not contain as much energy as a spirit medicine could hold.

But if his plan succeeded, he might truly be granted even a piece of the World Tree fruit.

The Blood Swordmasters paid respects at Jeong Yeon-shin's departure.

The Seventh Apostle followed with a smile.

Jeong Yeon-shin answered with an indifferent expression. The Seventh Apostle beamed.

* * *

Time passed.

Two fortnights came and went. Sunset and dawn crossed more than thirty times. The Blood Flame Sect members had grown quite accustomed to the young Grand Teacher.

Jeong Yeon-shin repeated the same routine for a month.

Railings, stairs, rafters, bare earth, rooftops—he roamed throughout the Blood Flame Sect, planting the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm.

He kept his movements centered mainly around the Sect Leader's hall.

He also studied various formations, including the Two Flame Blood Spirit Formation.

'Unless activated, formation mediums are merely still objects. Even martial artists cannot sense them. The same holds true for the Sect Leader.'

It might be different for the elf clans.

However, there were no elf clan members in the Blood Flame Sect. Their kind seemed to be intrinsically tied to the imperial court from birth.

Meanwhile, the Seventh Apostle was ordered out on an expedition.

She came to him with a face flushed with excitement. The killing intent coursing through her body was fierce.

Jeong Yeon-shin replied without inflection, seated cross-legged.

'It is beginning.'

He had felt it during today's outing. The Blood Flame Sect main hall was stirring.

Martial artists were searching the surrounding areas. The scale was considerable.

The Seventh Apostle's eyes narrowed as she spoke.

She extended her pale hand.

Her long fingers stroked Jeong Yeon-shin's hair, with truly formidable power breathing in every joint.

The heavily emanating energy waves were tremendous.
The corners of the Seventh Apostle's mouth rose.

Even to Jeong Yeon-shin, their sorcery formation transcended the human realm.

Just by gaining insight into part of its structure he could exploit formation key points, so the Blood Flame Sect's absolute trust in it was understandable.

These were people backed by a thousand-year supreme formation. He needed to understand their schemes.

He fixed his gaze on the Seventh Apostle and spoke.

There was a possessiveness in her reaching hands that treated him as an object.

WOONG!

Jeong Yeon-shin raised the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm along his cultivation path.

Supreme masters' movements were refined and difficult to strike, but a landed blow could inflict fatal wounds.

Just as when he had taken her eye.

He stood, disgusted by the Seventh Apostle's wheedling. Though he had been here for some time, his loathing of the Blood Flame Sect had only deepened.

These people's staple was not food but human lives.

Every day they captured martial artists and commoners, drained their blood and true qi, then discarded the husks.

Since they even possessed medical arts to prevent plagues, they frequently burned the corpses.

Shaking his head inwardly, he organized what needed to be done.

'I requested a meeting with the Blood Flame Sect Leader.'

It was time to load today's Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm into the Sect Leader's hall.

The Seventh Apostle answered belatedly. Jeong Yeon-shin left the room without a reply.

The end was approaching. His intuition told him so.

He felt no particular emotion even as he faced unwelcome faces.

The Third Apostle's son, Gao Zong-xue, stroked his thick chin while blocking the path.

He was one who had worked hard to undermine Jeong Yeon-shin's position over the past month. He could not be taken lightly.

His accumulated cultivation was powerful enough to subdue ordinary Blood Swordmasters in ten moves.
The corners of his mouth rose slightly.
Perhaps because Jeong Yeon-shin had endured all provocations with indifference, the man's words and actions had recently grown brazen.

Jeong Yeon-shin said. The reply came as sound transmission.

-Kill yourself. You broken-string lowborn.

His words were peculiar. Everyone knew the Seventh Apostle kept the Grand Teacher close.

Did he intend to ambush while she was briefly away?

That was when it happened.

'Hm?'

A stream of wind, seemingly alive, fixed his collar and swept past.

The breeze brushing his ear seemed to whisper words.

It was a sensation he had felt before. During his first mission. Cheong Myeong's demonstration of body technique had approached as a mysterious wind carrying elf power.

Jeong Yeon-shin spoke flatly and turned away.

He did not care whether the man raged behind him or followed without turning back.

He headed for the outskirts. There was no hesitation in his steps.

He arrived at once.

WOONG-

It was the place where he had first encountered the Two Flame Blood Spirit Formation with the Seventh Apostle. A place with a mystical atmosphere, as if space itself rippled.

She was beyond that barrier.

He swallowed hard without meaning to.

He saw her immediately. The instant she entered his vision, she was engraved in his mind.

His lifeline. His salvation.

She was a woman wearing long, light green martial clothes that at first glance resembled palace attire.

Her head was slightly raised, her face in profile.

Her milky white skin emanated a smooth radiance.

The rest of the scenery seemed to lose its color. Sunlight formed a halo around the elegant line of her nose.

The vegetation swaying around her settled into stillness, as if becoming one body.

Her light green hair—as though spring itself had seeped into each strand—flowed long enough to cover her back, spreading an aura of mystery.

It was not a human presence. Though she had completely contained her inner power, her spirit was terrifying.

Finally.

She turned her head.

Green eyes, brilliantly holding the sun above, were revealed. They were eyes containing an overwhelming abyss.
